rationale: The citing paper note says this is a commentary that uses Hedgcock and Rao’s fMRI attraction-effect study as its central case, explicitly critiquing that study’s construct definitions, controls, and ROI interpretations (amygdala, ACC, DLPFC). The cited paper provides the core empirical mechanism claim (trade-off aversion via fMRI) that the commentary evaluates, so this is clearly more than a passing background mention.
